channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

item.title: Gravitational wave detectors can now 'autotune' signals to harmonize the heavens - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Gravitational wave detectors can now 'autotune' signals to harmonize the heavens  Phys.org
  2. New technique allows scientists to 'hear' black hole collisions  Sky News
  3. “This Wasn't Really Something We Were Expecting To Be Able To Do”: Black Hole Collisions Can Now Be Used To “Autotune” Gravitational Wave Detectors  IFLScience
  4. A stunning first black hole's “kick” heard through space and time  Neowin
  5. Gravitational wave detectors can now ‘auto-tune’ their signals  Asia Research News |

item.title: Scientists Discover Neanderthals Were Using Teeth as Weapons, Just Not Their Own - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
1. Scientists Discover Neanderthals Were Using Teeth as Weapons, Just Not Their Own  Indian Defence Review
  2. Neanderthals used rhinoceros teeth as tools for making stone implements, study finds  Archaeology News Online Magazine
  3. News - Neanderthals May Have Used Rhino Teeth as Tools  Archaeology Magazine
  4. Modern experiments suggest rhino teeth may have been part of Neanderthal toolkits  Phys.org
  5. Neandertals used rhinoceros teeth as tools  Science News

item.title: A history of containers, an ancient technology hundreds of thousands of years in the making - Phys.org
item.description:
1. A history of containers, an ancient technology hundreds of thousands of years in the making  Phys.org
  2. The story of the first human tool: the humble container  New Scientist
  3. The story of the first human tool that might not be a weapon, scientists say it was far more important  The Times of India

item.title: Cut marks on 1.6-million-year-old bones reveal early humans moved and processed meat in Kenya - Archaeology News Online Magazine
item.description:
1. Cut marks on 1.6-million-year-old bones reveal early humans moved and processed meat in Kenya  Archaeology News Online Magazine
  2. Cut marks on 1.6 million-year-old bones reveal early humans moved prized meat  Phys.org
  3. 1.6 Million-Year-Old Bones Reveal How Early Humans Hunted and Shared Their Food  Indian Defence Review
  4. Humans Have Been Obsessed With the Best Cuts of Meat for 1.6 Million Years  VICE
  5. New Research Shows How the Earliest Humans Got Their Meat  Gizmodo

item.title: In quantum gravity, the cosmological constant may behave similar to the quantum Hall effect - Phys.org
item.description: In quantum gravity, the cosmological constant may behave similar to the quantum Hall effect  Phys.org
